cases.desc: Plain 3x3 table with a header row (control case).

expected.note: baseline; a correct GFM table has header + separator + 2 data rows

cases.desc: Header cell spanning 2 columns (colspan=2) over a 3-column body.

expected.note: spanned header cell — does converter duplicate/blank the spanned column?

cases.desc: First column uses rowspan=2 to group two rows under one label.

expected.note: rowspan grouping — Markdown has no rowspan; does the label repeat, blank, or drop the second row?

cases.desc: Mixed colspan and rowspan (a merged top-left 2x2 block).

expected.note: 2D merge — the hardest span case for a flat pipe grid

cases.desc: A table nested inside one cell of an outer table.

expected.note: GFM cannot nest tables — does inner data survive as text, flatten, or vanish?

cases.desc: Very wide table: 24 columns x 2 data rows.

expected.note: width stress — are all 24 columns kept, and is the header/separator arity correct?

cases.desc: Table with NO header cells — every cell is a <td>.

expected.note: GFM REQUIRES a header row. Does the converter promote row 1 to header (losing it as data) or synthesize a blank header?

cases.desc: Malformed table: rows have different numbers of cells (2, 4, 3).

expected.note: ragged rows — does the converter pad, truncate to the first row's width, or emit invalid GFM?

cases.desc: Table with genuinely empty cells and whitespace-only cells.

expected.note: empty-cell preservation — are blanks kept as empty pipe cells so column alignment holds?

cases.desc:
Cells containing a
    list, a
    line break, and a link.

expected.note: block content in cells — a newline inside a GFM cell breaks the table; does the converter inline/escape it?

cases.desc: Right-to-left (Arabic) text in a table with dir=rtl.

expected.note: RTL preservation — is the Arabic text kept intact and are cells in logical order?

cases.desc: Cells whose text contains pipe '|', asterisks, and backticks (GFM-significant).

expected.adversarial: literal '|' inside cells must be escaped or the table corrupts
expected.note: does the converter escape in-cell pipes so the column count stays 2?
does caption survive? does tfoot merge into the body? multi-row header collapse?
